summary_one_line: Input must carry the Mach-O magic ce-fa-ed-fe at offset 0 so the Mach-O parser populates range maps reached by FindContaining.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The first four bytes must equal the Mach-O magic number ce fa ed fe (little-endian 0xFEEDFACE). Bloaty's format dispatch matches this magic to enter the Mach-O object reader, which calls AddRange / FindContaining repeatedly; without the magic match the input is routed to a different (e.g. WASM) parser whose path drives FindContaining only on the false side.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed begins with ce fa ed fe at offsets 0x00-0x03; the BYTE DIFF shows W concentrated on ce/fa/ed/fe (and a few ASCII M/Z/L/E/P/F) at those offsets, and the winners reach FindContaining 562 times and its callers far more, with the blocker true side taken (T=101/160).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Every loser-blocking seed begins with 00 61 73 6d (the WASM magic '.asm'), so the Mach-O reader is never entered; FindContaining is still invoked 1720 times on the L set but the blocker true side is taken 0 times (T=0 F=1720), i.e. the loser parser path never produces the empty-result condition the true branch encodes.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc intercept the format-magic comparison and log both operands into the CmpLogObserver buffer; I2SRandReplace then scans the input for the seed's leading bytes and substitutes the expected magic operand (ce fa ed fe) directly at offset 0. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace, so they must stumble onto the exact 4-byte magic by chance via havoc, which is why naive never resolves (0/10) and value_profile resolves only 1/10.

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs differ by the I2S technique and both name I2S as the delta.
The cmplog>naive pair is also an I2S delta.
The blocked side is the true branch of FindContaining's empty-result test.
All winner-resolving seeds start with the Mach-O magic ce fa ed fe at offset 0.
The W byte set at offsets 0x00-0x03 is concentrated on ce/fa/ed/fe while the L byte set is the WASM magic 00 61 73 6d.
Every loser-blocking seed starts with 00 61 73 6d (the WASM '.asm' magic).
On the loser set FindContaining's blocker true side is taken 0 times despite 1720 invocations.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which scans the input for an operand and substitutes the other operand at matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S mutator, so its only route to the exact magic is chance havoc.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: naive never resolved the branch and value_profile resolved it only once across ten trials.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 141 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed 0cc6115ee776f4d4`; the 40-level ancestor chain contains two I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depth 38 (011297053b582f6d) and depth 39 (a24245b7a5bd58f1), the lower-bound signal of an I2SRandReplace find in vpc under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness that gates FindContaining behind a 4-byte magic compare shows naive resolving the true branch as often and as fast as cmplog/vpc (no I2S advantage), meaning the magic match is not the controlling feature.

weakest_evidence_point: The claim that the Mach-O magic at offset 0 is the specific gate (rather than some other 4-byte constant elsewhere in the header) is inferred from the seed-prefix divergence, not from a directly cited source-level magic comparison.
